content: The ICO Guide to UK GDPR DPIA outlines the following phases of a DPIA: (1) identify whether a DPIA is needed, (2) describe the processing and data flows, (3) consult stakeholders, (4) assess necessity and proportionality, (5) identify privacy risks, (6) define mitigating controls, (7) document decisions and sign-off, and (8) review outcomes after implementation.
source: ICO Guide to UK GDPR DPIA

content: The EDPB Guidelines on DPIA describe these phases: preliminary screening, processing description and purpose analysis, risk assessment for data subjects, selection of safeguards, decision on residual risk and consultation where required, and periodic review.

ICO Guide to UK GDPR DPIA phases: 1) Identify if a DPIA is needed, 2) Describe processing/data flows, 3) Consult stakeholders, 4) Assess necessity and proportionality, 5) Identify privacy risks, 6) Define mitigating controls, 7) Document decisions and sign-off, 8) Review outcomes after implementation.
source: ICO Guide to UK GDPR DPIA

content: EDPB Guidelines on DPIA phases: 1) Preliminary screening, 2) Processing description and purpose analysis, 3) Risk assessment for data subjects, 4) Selection of safeguards, 5) Decision on residual risk and consultation where required, 6) Periodic review.

The ICO's DPIA phases begin with determining if a DPIA is required, then describing the planned data processing and flows. Stakeholders are consulted to gather input. The necessity and proportionality of the processing are assessed to ensure compliance. Privacy risks are identified, and controls to mitigate these risks are defined. All decisions are documented and signed off, and the outcomes are reviewed after implementation.
source: ICO Guide to UK GDPR DPIA

content: The EDPB's DPIA phases start with a preliminary screening to check if a DPIA is needed. The next steps involve describing the processing and its purposes, assessing risks to data subjects, and selecting safeguards. A decision is made on the residual risk, and consultation is performed as required. The DPIA is periodically reviewed to ensure ongoing compliance.
